title: Add Tools to Your Agent
lede: Tools turn your Smart Agent from a talker into a doer — it can end calls gracefully, transfer angry callers to a human, book appointments on your calendar, press IVR digits, and even call your own APIs mid-conversation. This guide walks the whole Tools & MCP panel, tool by tool.

steps.title: Open Your Agent
steps.content:
Everything in this guide happens inside the agent editor, so first open the agent you want to power up.

  1. In the left sidebar, under Intelligence Center, click Smart Agents.
  2. Click the name of the agent you want to edit. The agent editor opens.

images.alt: Smart Agents list with agents to open
images.caption: Intelligence Center → Smart Agents — click an agent's name to open the editor.

images.alt: Smart Agents list with agents to open
images.caption: Intelligence Center → Smart Agents — click an agent's name to open the editor.

steps.title: Switch to Form View
steps.content:
The agent editor has two views — a visual Node canvas and a classic Form view. The Tools panel described here lives in the Form view.

  1. In the top toolbar, click Form on the Form / Node switch (if the editor opened on the node canvas).
  2. Look at the bottom of the prompt card on the left — you'll see three buttons: Sub Agents / Tasks / Handoffs, Tools / MCP, and Settings.
💡 Tip: The Tools / MCP button shows a live counter — for example (3) — so you can see at a glance how many tools this agent already has. In the Node view the same tools appear under the Agent node's Tools capability row, so nothing is lost when you switch views.

images.alt: Agent editor Form view with the Tools / MCP button at the bottom of the prompt card
images.caption: The Form view — the Tools / MCP button sits at the bottom of the prompt card.

images.alt: Agent editor Form view with the Tools / MCP button at the bottom of the prompt card
images.caption: The Form view — the Tools / MCP button sits at the bottom of the prompt card.

steps.title: Open the Tools & MCP Panel
steps.content:
1. Click Tools / MCP. The Tools & MCP panel opens.
  2. Get oriented — the panel has two halves:
    • Left rail (the catalog): three sections — Predefined (built-in actions), Custom (your reusable HTTP tools), and MCP Servers. Click any item to attach it.
    • Right panel (what's attached): every tool and MCP server this agent can use, newest first, with an items attached counter at the top. Click a card's header to expand its settings; click the trash icon to detach it.
  3. Each attached card is tagged so you always know what you're looking at: PREBUILT / CUSTOM / MCP type tags, plus a Main tag (and a gold tag per sub-agent Task that also uses the tool).
💡 Tip: Changes are saved as you make them — attaching, detaching, and editing settings all persist immediately. The Save button in the footer simply lights up to confirm you changed something.

images.alt: Tools and MCP panel with the catalog on the left and attached tools on the right
images.caption: The Tools & MCP panel — catalog on the left, attached tools (with type tags) on the right.

images.alt: Tools and MCP panel with the catalog on the left and attached tools on the right
images.caption: The Tools & MCP panel — catalog on the left, attached tools (with type tags) on the right.

steps.title: Meet the Predefined Tools
steps.content:
The Predefined section is your built-in action library — one click each to attach:

  • End Call — lets the agent hang up politely when the caller says goodbye (instead of talking forever).
  • SIP Transfer — hands the call off to an outside phone number (a human agent, another department, an answering service).
  • Press Digit (IVR Navigation) — the agent presses phone-menu digits, perfect for outbound calls that hit "press 1 for sales" menus.
  • Calendar — books real appointments straight into your CRM calendar, with conflict handling and optional job dispatch.
  • Internal Transfer — rings a team of your own users (a permission group) inside iMash; the first person to answer takes the call.
  1. Click a tool in the rail to attach it. A blue count badge appears next to it, and the new card pops open on the right, ready to configure.
  2. Click the same rail item again if you want a second instance (for example two SIP Transfers to different departments) — each instance gets a unique name like transfer_call_1, transfer_call_2.
⚠️ Heads-up: Every tool's name must be unique for the AI — iMash numbers them automatically, so don't rename two tools to the same thing.

images.alt: Catalog rail with Predefined and Custom tool sections
images.caption: The catalog rail — Predefined actions on top, your Custom tools below. Blue badges show how many instances are attached.

images.alt: Catalog rail with Predefined and Custom tool sections
images.caption: The catalog rail — Predefined actions on top, your Custom tools below. Blue badges show how many instances are attached.

steps.title: Configure a Transfer (Example: SIP Transfer)
steps.content:
Expand an attached tool's card to reveal its settings. Here's every field on the SIP Transfer card:

  • Description — the most important field on any tool: it tells the AI when to use it (e.g. "When the user is angry or requests a human agent, transfer the call").
  • Select Phone Number — which of your iMash numbers places the transfer leg (search by number or label).
  • Play Ringtone During Transfer — the caller hears ringing instead of silence while the transfer connects.
  • Use SIP REFER (Cold Transfer) — hands the call fully to the carrier and drops the AI off the line.
  • Transfer to — the destination phone number.
  • Post-Transfer Monitoring Settings — after a warm transfer the AI stays on the line: Disable STT After Transfer stops transcription to save cost, and Dual-Party Transcription transcribes both sides separately (higher STT cost).
  • Agent To Speaks Before Transfering The Call — toggle on and write a Say line (e.g. "One moment, connecting you now…") so the caller is never left hanging.

The Internal Transfer tool is configured the same way, but instead of a phone number you pick a Permission Group (plus optional Skills filters), choose Ring or Queue mode, set a No-Answer Timeout, and choose what the caller hears while waiting — ringtone, hold music, or silence.

images.alt: SIP Transfer tool card expanded showing all transfer settings
images.caption: The SIP Transfer card expanded — description, destination, ringtone, cold transfer, and monitoring options.

images.alt: SIP Transfer tool card expanded showing all transfer settings
images.caption: The SIP Transfer card expanded — description, destination, ringtone, cold transfer, and monitoring options.

steps.title: Turn On Appointment Booking (Calendar Tool)
steps.content:
The Calendar tool is a favorite: your agent checks the calendar and books real appointments while the caller is still on the line.

  1. Attach Calendar from the Predefined rail and expand its card.
  2. Under Enabled Calendar Categories, select which calendar categories the agent may book into.
  3. Open Booking Behavior and tune:
    • Booking Duration (mins) — default appointment length.
    • Allow Double Booking — leave ON to allow overlapping slots, or turn OFF to reveal conflict handling: what to say when a time is taken, whether to let the caller try another time, and whether to Offer close available time slots (with a suggestion window and max suggestions).
  4. Open Agent Speech Overrides to script exactly what the agent says before/after a successful or failed booking.
  5. Open Dispatch and toggle Create Job on Booking if every booked appointment should also create a scheduled job (optionally pre-setting job type, priority, country, and estimated cost).

images.alt: Calendar tool card expanded showing categories and booking behavior
images.caption: The Calendar tool — categories, booking duration, double-booking rules, speech overrides, and job dispatch.

images.alt: Calendar tool card expanded showing categories and booking behavior
images.caption: The Calendar tool — categories, booking duration, double-booking rules, speech overrides, and job dispatch.

steps.title: Attach Your Own API Tools (Custom)
steps.content:
Custom tools are HTTP calls to your systems — check an order status, look up a warranty, create a ticket — and the agent can fire them live, mid-conversation.

  1. In the Custom section of the rail, click any tool from your library to attach it. Attached tools show a green Added badge (each custom tool can be attached once per agent).
  2. The attached card shows a quick preview — Description, Method (GET/POST), and Endpoint URL.
  3. Click Edit Tool on the card to open the full editor and set the name, description, URL, parameters schema, and test the call.
  4. Need a brand-new one? Click Add Custom Tool at the bottom of the Custom section — iMash creates it, attaches it, and you can build it out from there.
💡 Tip: Ownership tags (Main Account / Sub User) show who created each tool in the library, which is handy on accounts with many sub-users. Full build instructions live in the Custom Tools guide (linked below).

images.alt: Attached custom tool card with method, endpoint, and Edit Tool button
images.caption: An attached custom tool — preview of method and endpoint, with Edit Tool for the full editor.

images.alt: Attached custom tool card with method, endpoint, and Edit Tool button
images.caption: An attached custom tool — preview of method and endpoint, with Edit Tool for the full editor.

steps.title: Connect an MCP Server (Optional)
steps.content:
MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers bundle whole toolboxes your agent can discover on its own — one connection can add dozens of abilities.

  1. Scroll to the MCP Servers section of the rail. Any servers you registered under Automation appear here — click one to attach it (it shows an Attached badge).
  2. Or click Add custom MCP server and fill in:
    • Name — a label for your team.
    • Server URL — the endpoint; URLs ending in /sse use SSE transport, /mcp use streamable HTTP (detected automatically).
    • Auth header (optional) — header name (defaults to Authorization) and value, e.g. Bearer <token>.
  3. Click Attach. The server appears as a card on the right; expand it to review the URL, or hit the trash icon and confirm Detach to remove it.

images.alt: Add custom MCP server dialog with name, URL, and auth header fields
images.caption: Add custom MCP server — name, server URL, and an optional auth header.

images.alt: Add custom MCP server dialog with name, URL, and auth header fields
images.caption: Add custom MCP server — name, server URL, and an optional auth header.

steps.title: How the Agent Decides to Use a Tool
steps.content:
You never script "call tool X at minute 2." Instead, the AI reads each tool's name and Description during the conversation and decides on its own when a tool fits — exactly like a human agent deciding when to transfer a call. That makes descriptions your steering wheel:

  • Describe the trigger, not the mechanics. "When the caller confirms they want an appointment, book it" beats "books calendar events."
  • Be specific about conditions. "Only transfer if the caller explicitly asks for a human or becomes upset" prevents trigger-happy transfers.
  • Mention the tool in your prompt when it matters. If the agent's main job is booking, tell it so in the system prompt — the tool description and prompt work together.
  • Use the speak toggles to cover silence. Tools take a second or two to run; "Agent speaks before/during execution" lines ("Let me check that for you…") keep the call feeling alive.

steps.title: Test Every Tool Before Going Live
steps.content:
The payoff moment — hear your agent actually do things.

  1. Click Test Agent in the editor's top toolbar (or use the chat panel on the right of the Form view) and click Connect.
  2. Say things that should trigger each tool, one at a time:
    • "I'd like to book an appointment tomorrow at 2 PM" → Calendar (then check the CRM calendar for the event).
    • "I want to speak to a real person" → SIP / Internal Transfer.
    • "That's all, goodbye!" → End Call.
  3. If a tool fires too eagerly (or never fires), sharpen its Description and test again — small wording changes make a big difference.
⚠️ Warning: Transfer tools place real calls during tests — point Transfer to at your own phone while testing, not at a live queue.

support.content:
